title: Crispy Tangy Spanish Oranges Soup
description: A bright, refreshing soup with orange zest and crunchy chickpeas, perfect for a monsoon-inspired lunch.
instructions: Wash and zest one orange, then juice the other to obtain 1 tbsp of juice. Slice the remaining orange into segments.
Combine the orange zest, orange juice, smoked paprika, cayenne pepper, and a pinch of salt in a small bowl; set aside.
Heat the olive oil in a medium saucepan over medium heat. Add the sliced onion and diced carrot, sautéing until the onion is translucent, about 3‑4 minutes.
Stir in the minced garlic and diced bell pepper; cook for another 2 minutes until fragrant.
Pour in the vegetable broth, bring to a gentle simmer, and add the orange mixture. Let the soup simmer for 8 minutes to meld flavors.
While the soup simmers, quickly fry the chickpeas in a small amount of oil until golden and crisp, then drain on paper towels.
Taste the soup, adjust seasoning with salt if needed, and ladle into bowls.
Garnish each serving with orange segments, a sprinkle of fresh cilantro, and a handful of crispy fried chickpeas before serving.
